name: I went back to work but got hurt again—what do I do now?
description: Report the re-injury immediately. It is typically handled as a continuation of your original claim. You should return to your doctor to get updated restrictions

mainEntity.name: I went back to work but got hurt again—what do I do now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report the re-injury immediately. It is typically handled as a continuation of your original claim. You should return to your doctor to get updated restrictions and ensure your wage benefits are restarted if you cannot continue working.
acceptedAnswer.description:
If you re-injure the same body part after returning to work, it is often treated as a 'recurrence' or an 'aggravation' of the original claim. You should report it immediately and return to your original workers' comp doctor. Do not let the employer tell you it's a 'new' injury that requires a new waiting period if it's clearly related to the first incident.

A re-injury often suggests that you were sent back to work too soon or that the 'light duty' was too strenuous.

- The insurance company for the first injury is usually still liable.
- New restrictions should be issued by your physician immediately.
- You may need to revisit your disability rating (PPD).

Document exactly what task you were doing when the pain returned. If your employer tries to treat it as a brand-new accident to avoid higher premiums, legal counsel can help link the two incidents to ensure continuous coverage.
